intro: Warneckea urschii, also known as Uruhu or simply Warneckea, is a medicinal plant species belonging to the family Melastomataceae. The plant has been traditionally used in East African folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, due to its analgesic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Warneckea urschii include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Warneckea is often prepared as a tea or infusion, and sometimes used in combination with other herbs to treat various health conditions.
